title: pellaea involuta
intro: Pellaea involuta is a type of fern, specifically a species of the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Pellaea involuta has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering potential benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used depending on the desired application.
